titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/224

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=176 status=live modified=2018-01-17 01:10:07.490749+00 | translation moa=177 status=live modified=2017-01-27 09:40:06.824685+00

div.p:
M. Rex Scott’. Iusticiis suis . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . Cunctisque
aliis probis hominibus totius terre sue . salutem . Mando ⁊ firmiter precipio ut in cuiuscumque terra aut potestate Abbas de Sancta Cruce inuenire poterit Natiuos
homines terre sue aut fugitiuos suos  eos iuste habeat . Nullusque ei aut ser
uienti suo quemquam ex illis iniuste detineat  super meam plenariam forisfacturam . Testibus Ern’ Episcopo de Sancto Andrea . Iohanne abbate de Calchou . Galfr’ abbate de Dunferml’ .
Apud Castellum puellarum.

Malcolm king of Scots to his Justices, Sheriffs, Provosts, Officials, and all other worthy men of the whole of his land, Greeting: I command and firmly enjoin that in whosesoever land or power the abbot of Holyrood is able to find neyfs of his own land or his own fugitives, he should rightly have them. And no one may withhold any of them from him or his servant, on pain of my full forfeiture. As witnesses, Arnold bishop of St Andrews, John abbot of Kelso, Geoffrey abbot of Dunfermline. At Maidens’ Castle.
